tiktok.caption: You've been paying your credit card on the wrong day your whole life — and it just cost you $21,000 on your mortgage

youtube_short.title: Pay Your Credit Card Wrong Day = Lose $21K
title_alternatives: The Credit Card Payment Timing Mistake That Costs $21,000
Most People Pay Their Credit Card on the Wrong Day
youtube_short.description: Almost everyone pays their credit card on the due date — but your credit score is calculated days earlier, on your statement close date. If you pay after that date, bureaus already saw your high balance. Being above 30% utilization can cost you 50 credit score points. On a 30-year mortgage, that 50-point swing adds $21,000 in interest. The fix: find your statement close date in your card app and pay down your balance BEFORE that date — not the due date. Your score updates in 30 days. #creditcard #creditscore #personalfinance Follow for daily money psychology breakdowns.

hook_line: You've been paying on the wrong day
